rationale: The 2003 paper is cited only in a grouped literature-review citation (with the 2006 follow-up and other studies) as part of prior work on information-source choice, time spent, and perceived helpfulness. In the citing paper, this serves as broad background to position a gap, namely judging relative value across individual providers in stranger-to-stranger online exchanges. The cited paper is not given a distinct role beyond that stream-level mention, and the citing study does not directly reuse its model, measures, or empirical design.
